title: Error Indicator Readout - DINGLI JCPT0607DCS Operator's Manual Page 31 | ManualsLib
content_summary: Dingli JCPT0607DCS full fault code list (operator's manual page 31): Code 10 GENERAL ALARM ECU; Code 20 GENERAL ALARM TM1; Code 30 GENERAL ALARM PCU; Code 31 PCU CPU0 FAULT; Code 32 PCU CPU1 FAULT; Code 51 ECU ALARM; Code 52 PCU ALARM; Code 53 TM1 ALARM; Code 54 PRESS SENSOR ERROR — check pressure sensor wiring and sensor; Code 57 OVERLOAD — platform load exceeds rated capacity, reduce load; Code 58 POTHOLE — pothole protection activated, drive machine over pothole/edge slowly; Code 59 HIGH POSITION LIMIT — platform at maximum height limit; Code 110 ECU WATCHDOG FAULT — ECU internal error, restart machine; Code 111 CANBUS TIMEOUT 1 PCU CPU0 — communication fault between PCU and ECU, check CAN bus wiring; Code 112 CANBUS TIMEOUT 1 PCU CPU1 — same as 111 for CPU1 channel. Reset procedure: push in and pull out the red Emergency Stop button.

title: How to Fix Dingli JCPT0607A Fault Code 69 — Power Relay Sleep
content_summary: Dingli JCPT0607A fault code 69 — Power Relay Sleep: The system entered sleep mode and the power relay was deactivated. This code also appears on JCPT1912DC. Cause: battery charge too low to maintain power relay energized, or key switch issue, or prolonged inactivity triggering sleep. Solutions: (1) Restart the machine by cycling the key switch off and back on. (2) Check battery charge level — charge batteries fully before operating. (3) Verify key switch operation and wiring. (4) Check power relay and its control circuit. If machine powers up and immediately shuts down, battery may be too low to sustain operation; fully charge before next use.

title: How to Fix Dingli JCPT1912DC Fault Code 69 — Power Relay Sleep
content_summary: Dingli JCPT1912DC fault code 69 — Power Relay Sleep: Same as JCPT0607A code 69. System entered sleep mode and power relay deactivated. Solutions: (1) Restart via key switch cycle. (2) Fully charge batteries. (3) Verify key switch and power relay. The JCPT1912DC is a DC-powered 19ft scissor lift. If error persists after full charge, inspect the power relay coil and control wiring from the ECU. Check for corrosion on battery terminals and main power connections.

title: Error Indicator Readout; List Of Fault Codes - DINGLI JCPT0307 Operators Manual Page 29 | ManualsLib

title: Faults Scissor Lift Aerial Work Platform and Solutions | Cathay Lift
content_summary: Scissor lift aerial work platform common faults and solutions guide. Hydraulic system faults: (1) Platform won't lift or lifts slowly — check hydraulic oil level, inspect for leaks, check pump pressure, verify solenoid valve operation; (2) Platform drifts down when raised — check cylinder seals, check load holding valve, inspect hydraulic hose connections; (3) Uneven lifting — check for air in hydraulic system, bleed hydraulic lines, inspect cylinder synchronization. Electrical faults: (1) Machine won't power on — check battery charge, inspect key switch, check main fuse/circuit breaker; (2) Controls unresponsive — check control harness connections, inspect joystick, verify ECU power. Mechanical faults: (1) Scissor arm noise — inspect pivot pins and bushings, lubricate; (2) Machine won't drive — check drive motor, inspect drive controller, verify safety systems (tilt, pothole guard). Maintenance tips: weekly oil inspection, monthly greasing of all pivot points, annual hydraulic oil change.

title: Hydraulic Aerial Lifts Maintenance 101 | Cylinders, Inc.
content_summary: Technical guide to preventing hydraulic problems in boom lift aerial work platforms. Key maintenance topics: (1) Hydraulic fluid — use correct grade (typically ISO 46 hydraulic oil), change every 1000 hours or annually, check level weekly; contamination causes premature pump and valve failure. (2) Cylinder seals — inspect for leaks at rod and barrel, replace if weeping; platform drift indicates internal cylinder leak or load holding valve fault. (3) Hoses and fittings — inspect for abrasion, cracking, chafing; replace hoses every 5-7 years regardless of appearance. (4) Hydraulic pump — listen for cavitation noise (whining), check inlet filter/strainer; cavitation causes rapid pump damage. (5) Control valves — solenoid valves: test coil resistance (typically 20-40 ohms), check for contamination sticking valve spool. (6) Pressure settings — main relief valve, secondary relief valves; check against service manual specifications. Diagnostic approach: use pressure gauges at test ports shown on hydraulic schematic.

title: Diagnosing AWP That Won't Lift: Electrical, Hydraulic, And Lockout Causes | Atomoving
content_summary: Technical guide for diagnosing aerial work platforms that won't lift. Systematic diagnosis approach: Step 1 Lockout causes — check E-stop status, confirm all interlocks satisfied (outriggers deployed if required, tilt sensor not triggered, weight within rated capacity, height limit not reached), verify key switch on. Step 2 Electrical causes — measure battery voltage under load (must meet manufacturer minimum), check control harness continuity, verify pump motor relay operation and motor current draw (stalled motor draws high current). Step 3 Hydraulic causes — check fluid level at reservoir, test pump outlet pressure at test port using pressure gauge (compare to rated pressure in service manual), verify solenoid valve coil resistance (typical 20-40Ω), check solenoid spool movement, perform cylinder internal leak test (raise platform, isolate circuit, watch for drift). Step 4 Read fault codes — use manufacturer analyzer tool or observe LED display. Covers Dingli JCPT series, JLG scissor and boom lifts, Genie GS series.

title: JLG 450AJ Fault Code 3:3 — Platform Troubleshooting | JustAnswer
content_summary: JustAnswer expert discussion about JLG 450AJ fault code 3:3 on the platform control. Fault code 3:3 on JLG 450AJ indicates a short circuit in an output driver — there are at least 50 possible troubleshooting paths for this code. Diagnosis steps: (1) Inspect basket position sensors for dirt, damage, or loose wiring; (2) Check joystick calibration; (3) Ensure all connectors are secure; (4) Reset system by cycling power. Advanced diagnosis: check power and control wiring for shorts in harness and connector pins; if harness tests OK, the controller requires replacement. Note: fault code 3:3 is hardware-related and can originate from any part of the machine. Service manual section for DTC group 3 covers all platform and boom positioning faults. Field tech tip: isolate the issue by disconnecting platform harness and checking if code clears (narrows fault to platform vs. base machine).

title: JLG Fault Codes List, Diagnosis, and Repair | Friday Parts
content_summary: JLG fault codes reference covering DTCs for boom lifts and scissor lifts with diagnosis and repair procedures. JLG DTC structure: first digits indicate system group, remaining digits indicate specific fault within that group. Troubleshooting process: (1) Identify DTC from machine display or analyzer; (2) Refer to service manual DTC table for that model; (3) Follow diagnostic flowchart; (4) Perform corrective action (inspect wiring, replace component, calibrate sensor); (5) Clear code and verify repair. Common repair procedures: wiring harness inspection (check for chafing, corrosion, broken conductors at flex points), connector cleaning and re-pinning, sensor replacement and calibration, controller replacement. Parts compatibility note: JLG controllers and sensors are model-specific — verify part numbers before ordering. Covers JLG 450AJ, 520AJ, 510AJ, 860SJ, and scissor lift models.

title: JLG Fault Codes — Complete Guide & What They Mean | EWP Spares Australia
content_summary: Complete guide to JLG fault codes from EWP Spares Australia, a specialist aerial work platform parts supplier. Covers fault code structure, reading procedures, and common codes with solutions. JLG Analyzer (Part 1001249695) provides full fault description, active and historical faults, and live sensor monitoring. Machine analyzer options: tethered cable, mobile handheld, Bluetooth (select scissor models), remote via ClearSky fleet management. Fault code categories: engine codes (Group 1), drive/travel codes (Group 2), boom/platform codes (Group 3), sensor codes (Group 4), CAN communication codes (Group 5), load management codes (Group 9). Specific codes: 5-437 CAN bus communication fault, 3-3 short circuit in output driver, 4-5 platform overload, 9-2/9-3 load management system faults. Reset: cycle emergency stop or key switch after resolving underlying fault. Applies to JLG 450AJ, 520AJ, 860SJ, and scissor lift models common in Australian market.

title: Detect & Fix Genie Error Codes (Troubleshooting) — BIBERGER
content_summary: Genie scissor lift and boom lift error code troubleshooting guide from Biberger. Covers Genie SmartLink Diagnostic System (GSDS) fault codes for GS series scissor lifts. Operation Indicator Codes (OIC) with E-prefix: E1 Emergency Stop, E2 Recovery Mode, E4 Low Battery, E6 Tilt Sensor, E7 Platform Overload, E12/E13 Communication fault. Diagnostic Trouble Codes (DTC) numeric: 18 Pothole guard, 42-47 solenoid coil faults, 68 Low Voltage. Troubleshooting steps for each code: E4 Low Battery — measure battery voltage (GS-1932: must be ≥22V, GS-3246: ≥22V), charge batteries, check battery connections and individual cell voltages; E6 Tilt — move machine to level surface, check tilt sensor wiring and mounting; Code 18 Pothole guard — inspect guard mechanism deployment and limit switch continuity. Battery voltage requirements by model family. German language site covering European market Genie equipment.

title: Genie GS 3246 Error Code 18 After Limit Switch Install | JustAnswer
content_summary: JustAnswer expert discussion about Genie GS-3246 showing error code 18 after limit switch replacement. Error code 18 = Pothole guard failure — pothole guard not deploying or limit switch not activating correctly. Diagnosis after switch replacement: (1) Verify wiring harness connections to new limit switch; (2) Inspect pothole guard sensor for damage or misalignment; (3) Reset system codes via ground control override; (4) Test limit switch continuity with multimeter (normally open/closed per wiring diagram); (5) Consider controller recalibration if new switch tests OK electrically. Common causes of persistent code 18 after repair: incorrect switch type installed (NO vs NC), harness connector not fully seated, pothole guard arm not returning to fully deployed position. Reference: Genie GS-3246 service manual wiring diagram for pothole guard circuit. Same procedure applies to GS-2046 and GS-2646 models.

title: Manitou Default Fault Codes — Official Manitou Diagnostic Portal
content_summary: Official Manitou Group diagnostic portal listing default fault codes for Manitou equipment including ATJ and aerial work platform series. The portal provides manufacturer-level fault code definitions, causes, and corrective actions for Manitou telehandlers and aerial work platforms. Covers Manitou 180ATJ and other ATJ series machines. Fault code categories: engine ECU codes (Deutz engine variants), hydraulic system codes, safety system codes, transmission codes. This is the official Manitou diagnostic reference — supersedes third-party interpretations. Manitou Maintenance & Diagnostic Software (MDS) required for full code reading and clearing; this portal provides the code reference list for field technicians. Key for 180ATJ: Deutz EURO 3 engine codes overlap with platform control codes — must distinguish between engine ECU faults and Manitou platform controller faults.

title: Genie Scissor Lift GS3246 2003 Model Fault Light Causes and Fixes | JustAnswer
content_summary: JustAnswer expert discussion about a 2003 Genie GS-3246 scissor lift showing fault light. The GS-3246 fault indicator light on the control panel signals an active DTC. Diagnosis steps for 2003 GS-3246 (older model with GEN 5 ECU): (1) Check ground control LED for fault code number; (2) Reference Genie service manual fault code chart for GEN 5; (3) Common faults on older GS-3246: charging system faults (code 52-58), solenoid coil failures (code 42-47 — coils weaken over time), pothole guard wear (code 18). Battery-related issues common on older machines: sulfated batteries causing low voltage codes, charger failure causing persistent low voltage. Maintenance insight: 2003-era Genie GS-3246 uses older hydraulic pump design — pump efficiency decreases with age, may show slow lift even without fault codes. Platform tilt sensor calibration may drift over time.

title: Dingli JCPT1412DC Manuals — Operator and Service Manuals | ManualsLib
content_summary: ManualsLib listing for Dingli JCPT1412DC self-propelled electric scissor lift manuals. Platform capacity: 230kg (indoor), lower for outdoor/elevated use per rated capacity chart. Dingli service contact for technical support listed in manual.

title: Manitou Fault Codes and Solutions Guide | Scribd PDF

title: JLG Control System Section 6 — CAN Bus Troubleshooting Manual (3121171)
content_summary: JLG control system troubleshooting manual section 6 (reference 3121171) covering the JLG CAN-bus control system used in boom lift models. This document provides in-depth troubleshooting for JLG electrical/control system architecture applicable to articulating booms (450AJ, 520AJ, 600AJ) and telescopic booms (800S, 860SJ) using the same CAN-bus platform. Section 6 covers: CAN bus wiring topology and node identification, controller module identification (Ground ECM, Platform ECM, Engine Control Module), diagnostic analyzer connection points, DTC reading and clearing via JLG Analyzer, controller replacement and programming procedures, CAN bus fault isolation methodology. title: Dingli JCPT1208AC Compact Scissor Lift - Official Product Page
content_summary: Dingli JCPT1208AC compact scissor lift official product page. title: Scissor Lift Fault Reset and Troubleshooting Guide for Industrial Users
content_summary: Comprehensive scissor lift fault reset and troubleshooting guide. Key diagnostic approach: distinguish electrical from hydraulic faults. Electrical fault indicators: platform controls dead, indicator lights off, E-stop latched. Hydraulic fault indicators: controls powered, solenoids click but lift fails to move or build pressure. Common hydraulic faults: worn seals/hoses, contaminated hydraulic fluid, low fluid levels, damaged cylinders/pumps. Fault reset procedure: locate and press emergency stop reset, verify hydraulic fluid level, check for fault indicator LEDs, cycle machine power. Hydraulic: check fluid reservoir, bleed air from system, inspect cylinder seals. Electrical: check control board fuses, harness connectors, battery charge. Applies to general scissor lift brands including Dingli, Genie, JLG.

title: Common Scissor Lift Problems: How to Prevent and Fix
content_summary: Common scissor lift problems with prevention and fix guidance. Problems covered: 1) Sudden loss of lifting power or failure to raise/lower — caused by worn seals, contaminated hydraulic fluid, low fluid level, damaged cylinders or pumps; fix by checking fluid levels, inspecting hoses and seals. 2) Uneven lifting — often caused by cylinder imbalance or stuck valves. 3) Drive system failure — check battery charge, motor brushes, drive controller. 4) Electrical failures — check fuses, relays, control boards. General maintenance advice: monitor hydraulic oil level, inspect hoses for leaks, check seals regularly. Applies to self-propelled scissor lifts generally.

title: MEC AWP Fault Codes Document 95811-SCH
content_summary: MEC AWP fault codes and error indicator readout document 95811-SCH. Contains fault code chart for MEC aerial work platforms. title: How to Fix Dingli JCPT0607A Fault Code 20 — General Alarm TM1
content_summary: Dingli JCPT0607A fault code 20 — General Alarm TM1 (Traction Motor 1): This code indicates a Traction Motor 1 controller fault, specifically a motor driver issue. Symptoms: machine may not drive, or drive erratically; platform may not respond to drive commands. Root cause: motor controller (TM1 module) has detected an internal fault or overtemperature condition. Diagnostic steps: (1) Check TM1 motor controller wiring and harness for loose connectors or corrosion; (2) Inspect motor driver module for visible damage or burnt components; (3) Verify battery voltage — low voltage can trigger false TM1 alarms; (4) Check for obstructions in drive wheels causing motor overload; (5) Allow machine to cool if overtemperature is suspected. Reset: push in and pull out red Emergency Stop button. If code returns immediately, TM1 controller may need replacement. ChinaLiftSupply certified technicians specialize in Dingli JCPT equipment diagnostics.

title: Manitou MT1440PE3 / MT1840PE3 Fault Codes DTC List | Scribd
content_summary: Complete DTC (Diagnostic Trouble Code) list for Manitou MT1440PE3 and MT1840PE3 telehandlers/AWP platforms. These machines share a similar fault code scheme with the 180ATJ articulated work platform. Fault code categories include transmission faults, hydraulic system faults, electrical system faults, sensor faults, and engine faults (where applicable). Common codes cover: lack of travel, air leaks in hydraulic circuit, oil leaks, fork/platform top-out or bottom-out errors, load sensor faults, tilt sensor faults, CAN bus communication errors, and emergency stop conditions. The DTC table identifies code number, fault description, possible cause, and recommended corrective action. This document is useful for cross-referencing codes seen on 180ATJ as the control architecture is closely related.

title: Dingli JCPT HD-DC Electric Scissor Lift Parts Manual | Scribd
content_summary: Parts manual for Dingli JCPT HD-DC series electric scissor lifts. Document is a Russian-language optimized PDF (parts are internationally numbered). Contains exploded parts diagrams, part numbers, and assembly references for: hydraulic system components (pump, cylinder, valve block), electrical system components (controller modules including ECU/PCU/TM1 modules, cables, solenoid valves), drive system (motor, gearbox, wheels), and structural components. The HD-DC series represents a higher-duty-cycle variant of Dingli JCPT electric scissors. Identifying correct part numbers from this manual is essential for controller module replacement (TM1, ECU, PCU) when fault codes indicate controller failure.

title: JLG 450AJ Series II Troubleshooting — Warning Lights, Blocking, 2-Speed Issues | JustAnswer
content_summary: JLG 450AJ Series II troubleshooting forum thread. User reports: machine always blocking, 2-speed not working, warning lights active. Expert guidance: (1) Warning light / MIL codes flash on platform console AND light on control board inside ground control box — both must be checked; (2) Flash code reading procedure: count primary flashes (before pause) and secondary flashes (after pause) to form code e.g. 3-3; (3) For 2-speed issues: check speed selector switch, hydraulic drive circuit pressure, and drive motor function; (4) Blocking faults: check angle/tilt sensors, platform position sensors, hydraulic pressure sensors; (5) Cycle E-stop to attempt reset; (6) For persistent blocking: use JLG analyzer tool to read and clear DTCs. Service manual for 450AJ Series II has fault code table starting page 343. Contact JLG technical support at jlg.com for code-specific guidance.

title: JLG Flash Fault Code — Motor Flash Codes 450AJ, 3246ES, E400AJPN | JustAnswer
content_summary: JLG aerial lift flash fault code troubleshooting for models 450AJ, 3246ES, E400AJPN. Machine displaying motor-related flash codes. Flash code interpretation: primary flash count (before long pause) indicates system; secondary count (after pause) indicates specific fault. Motor-related flash codes typically indicate drive motor controller faults, motor overtemperature, motor overcurrent, or motor wiring faults. Diagnostic procedure: (1) Note both flash counts precisely; (2) Consult fault flash codes table in service manual (E450A page 147, 450A Series II page 343); (3) Inspect drive motor wiring, connectors, and motor terminals; (4) Check motor controller (SCR or MOSFET drive module); (5) Verify battery voltage — low voltage causes false motor alarms; (6) Check for mechanical drag on drive wheels/motors. For model 3246ES: scissor-specific motor codes may relate to the lift motor circuit rather than drive motor.

title: Genie GS-1530/1532 GS-2032 GS-2632 GS-2046 Service and Repair Manual #1272217 | Scribd
content_summary: Genie service and repair manual #1272217 covering GS-1530, GS-1532, GS-2032, GS-2632, GS-2046 scissor lifts. Document contains: complete fault code tables (Diagnostic Trouble Codes and Operation Indicator Codes), hydraulic system schematics, electrical schematics, maintenance procedures, and repair instructions. The fault code section (from page 182) provides: code number, code description, possible causes, and corrective actions. OIC codes indicate operator-induced conditions (tilt, overreach). DTC codes indicate electrical/hydraulic system faults. Five monitored systems: hydraulic, battery/power, user interface, sensors/switches, and controls. This manual applies to GS series machines with the Genie Smart System (GSDS) onboard diagnostic system.

title: JLG Error Code 5:437 Troubleshooting | Forkliftaction Forum
content_summary: Forkliftaction forum discussion about JLG error code 5:437. Code 5:437 on JLG equipment indicates a fault in the control system, category 5 (typically related to output driver circuits or load management faults). Community technicians share diagnostic approaches: (1) Check the specific output circuit indicated by sub-code 437; (2) Inspect wiring harness to solenoid valves or drive motor outputs; (3) Verify ground circuits and power supply rails; (4) Use JLG analyzer to read detailed fault description; (5) Check for hydraulic valve coil resistance — open or shorted coils trigger code 5 series faults; (6) Clear codes and retest after wiring inspection. Forum discussion includes real-world field experience from mechanics. Forkliftaction.com is a specialist platform with verified industry technician contributors.

title: How to Maintain a Scissor Lift — Complete Maintenance Guide | LGLifter
content_summary: Comprehensive scissor lift maintenance guide covering all AWP brands. Key maintenance intervals and procedures: DAILY — visual inspection, battery charge check, fluid levels, E-stop function test, safety device verification; WEEKLY — hydraulic oil level, check for leaks (fittings, cylinders, hoses), tire/wheel inspection, control function test; MONTHLY — battery terminal cleaning, hydraulic filter check, scissor arm pivot pin lubrication, limit switch function; ANNUAL — full hydraulic fluid change, battery load test, structural inspection, safety valve testing. Fault prevention: hydraulic oil contamination causes 40% of hydraulic faults; replace filter at 500-hour intervals or annually. Battery maintenance: check electrolyte level (flooded type), equalize charge monthly. Common fault indicators requiring immediate attention: oil below minimum mark, abnormal noises during lift, uneven platform rise, any fault code display. Maintenance log keeping recommended for warranty compliance.

title: Hydraulic Scissor Lift Maintenance and Troubleshooting
content_summary: Comprehensive hydraulic fault diagnosis for scissor lifts. (1) Jerky/pulsating lifting — causes: low hydraulic oil, clogged suction/return filters, air trapped in circuit; solutions: refill with correct viscosity oil, clean/replace filters, bleed air from cylinders and lines. (2) Lift stalls at height — causes: overload, incorrect pressure relief valve setting; fix: verify load within rated capacity, adjust pressure relief valve to manufacturer specification. (3) Slow ascent or descent — causes: clogged strainers, contaminated control valves, insufficient fluid; fix: clean strainers, flush and replace hydraulic fluid. (4) Visible leaks — worn seals/hoses, contaminated fluid; fix: replace seals, inspect cylinders and hoses. Preventive maintenance: check hydraulic systems for leaks, maintain proper fluid levels, regular fluid changes reduce wear.

title: Scissor Lift Troubleshooting and Manual Operation Best Practices
content_summary: Best practices for scissor lift fault diagnosis and manual override operation. Covers: (1) Fault isolation — separate electrical from hydraulic causes first; check interlocks (E-stop, tilt sensor, overload), limit switches, batteries, chargers, then hydraulic circuits. (2) Manual lowering procedure: use manual descent valve on hydraulic manifold when power is unavailable. (3) Operator console diagnostics: LED flash codes indicate specific faults; count flash sequences. (4) Battery diagnostics: test voltage under load (not just resting voltage). (5) Charger fault diagnosis: check charger output voltage before blaming battery. Applies to JLG, Genie, Dingli, Manitou scissor lifts. Reset procedure: lower completely to ground, turn off, engage override mode if available, restart.

title: Essential Guide to Aerial Work Platform Troubleshooting
content_summary: Systematic AWP troubleshooting guide. Step-by-step fault-finding sequence: (1) Check E-stop buttons (platform and ground); (2) Verify interlock conditions — tilt sensor (requires level ground), overload sensor (platform weight), pothole guards (if equipped). (3) Inspect limit switches for damage or dirt. (4) Test battery voltage under load — minimum 22V for 24V systems. (5) Check charger connection and output. (6) Inspect hydraulic fluid level and pump operation. (7) Check motor controller fuses and connections. Systematic separation of electrical vs. hydraulic faults reduces diagnostic time. Applies to scissor lifts and boom lifts.

title: Common Scissor Lift Problems and How to Fix Them
content_summary: Practical guide to common scissor lift faults and repairs. Problems covered: (1) Machine won't lift — check hydraulic fluid level, inspect pump motor, verify solenoid valve operation; (2) Platform drifts down slowly — cylinder seal wear or faulty check valve; solution: replace cylinder seals or check valve cartridge; (3) Won't drive — drive motor controller fault, check motor connections and controller PWM output; (4) Tilt/overload fault — machine must be on level surface within 1.5° for tilt sensor to clear; (5) E-stop fault — pull out all red E-stop buttons, cycle power. Hydraulic oil: always use manufacturer-specified viscosity. Battery maintenance: equalize charge monthly on lead-acid batteries.

title: Scissor Lift Electrical Troubleshooting | ElectricianTalk Forum
content_summary: Technical forum discussion on scissor lift electrical fault diagnosis. Key topics: (1) Motor controller testing — check input voltage, PWM signal output, and fault LED indicators on controller PCB; (2) Wiring harness inspection — look for chafing at articulation points, connector pin corrosion (especially at boom connections on outdoor machines); (3) Battery voltage drop under load — test with platform fully loaded and operating; acceptable drop is <2V for 24V system; (4) Interlock circuit tracing — E-stop, tilt, overload wired in series; use multimeter continuity test; (5) CAN bus errors — check termination resistors (120 ohm at each end). Applicable to Genie and JLG electric scissor lifts.

title: Lift Table Faults, Causes and Possible Solutions
content_summary: Systematic fault table for hydraulic lift equipment. Faults and solutions: (1) Lift won't start — check E-stop button, power supply voltage, fuses/circuit breakers; (2) Slow lifting speed — hydraulic pump wear, low fluid level, clogged filter; solution: check and refill fluid, replace filter, inspect pump output pressure; (3) Won't lower — solenoid valve stuck closed; solution: check solenoid coil resistance (normal 20-30 ohm), replace if open circuit; (4) Jerky/uneven motion — air in hydraulic system; solution: bleed air via repeated full cycles with load; (5) Platform won't hold height — internal cylinder bypass, faulty check valve; (6) Hydraulic leak — identify source (seal, hose, fitting); replace seals with manufacturer-specified kit.

title: JLG 450AJ Series 1 (Ford Engine) — Hydraulic Control Valve Loss | Forkliftaction Forum
content_summary: Technical forum discussion on JLG 450AJ Series 1 hydraulic control valve failures. Root cause identified: connector terminals corrode inside harness connectors on aged outdoor machines, causing intermittent or complete loss of electrical signal to hydraulic control solenoid valves. Symptoms: specific functions (boom up, swing, etc.) fail intermittently or permanently. Diagnostic approach: (1) Use JLG handset (service tool) to read active fault codes and test individual outputs; (2) Inspect harness connectors at boom connection points — pull apart and check for green/white corrosion on pins; (3) Clean connectors with electrical contact cleaner; (4) Replace corroded connectors if cleaning fails. Note: hydraulic pump, valves, and cylinders typically good — electrical connector corrosion is primary culprit on aged 450AJ. CAN bus communication errors may also present.

title: Genie GS-1530/1532 Operator's Manual — Serial GS30P-200101+
content_summary: Official Genie operator's manual for GS-1530 and GS-1532 scissor lifts. Contents: safety rules, pre-operation inspection checklist, operating procedures, emergency procedures, maintenance schedules, specification tables. Fault indication: if LED readout displays error code, push in and pull out red Emergency Stop button to reset system. SmartLink diagnostic indicator codes explained. Platform/ground control selector operation. Manual descent procedure using hand pump or manual lowering valve location. Battery charging requirements and indicator lights. Covers serial numbers from GS30P-200101 onwards.

title: Genie S-40 XC™ Service and Repair Manual (1286829GT)
content_summary: Official Genie service and repair manual for S-40 XC stick boom lift (serial S40XCH-101+). Covers: complete fault code diagnostics including DTC chart, hydraulic system service procedures (pump, valve, cylinder), electrical system troubleshooting (wiring diagrams, CAN bus), boom and platform calibration procedures, JIB boom servicing, turntable and swing system. SmartLink diagnostic system: fault codes displayed on LCD, stored in ECM memory. Reset procedures for calibration faults. Preventive maintenance intervals. Hydraulic pressure settings and adjustment procedures.

title: Dingli JCPT0607DCS Manuals Index — ManualsLib
content_summary: ManualsLib product index page for Dingli JCPT0607DCS scissor lift, listing all available documentation. Multiple manual revisions available for different serial number ranges. Useful index for locating correct Dingli JCPT0607DCS documentation version.

title: JLG 20MVL Fault Code 343 — Technical Troubleshooting | Forkliftaction Forum
content_summary: Technical forum discussion on JLG 20MVL vertical mast lift fault code 343. Diagnosis: fault 343 on JLG vertical mast lifts typically relates to platform tilt/level sensor circuit. Troubleshooting steps: (1) Check platform angle sensor wiring and connector; (2) Test sensor output voltage (should vary smoothly with tilt angle); (3) Inspect wiring harness for damage between sensor and control board; (4) If sensor and wiring OK, replace control board. General JLG fault code approach: warning indicators flash on both platform console and inside ground control box — count flash sequences for specific code. Applicable to JLG vertical mast lift models.

title: JLG Expert Troubleshooting Discussion — Flash Codes and Diagnostics | Forkliftaction Forum
content_summary: Forum discussion on JLG boom lift diagnostic approach and flash code interpretation. Key information: (1) JLG machines use flash code system — light on platform console and control board inside ground control box flash MIL codes; count number of flashes in sequence; (2) Flash code format: first sequence = category, second sequence = specific fault (e.g., 3 flashes then 3 = fault 3-3); (3) Common flash codes: 3-3 (sensor fault — angle/level sensor), 4-4 (drive system fault); (4) JLG handset tool (service analyzer) required for full diagnostic data on newer machines; (5) Wiring harness connector corrosion is leading cause of flash codes on older boom lifts. Covers JLG 450AJ, 450A, E450AJ, and related articulating boom lifts.

title: How to Fix Dingli JCPT0607A Fault Code 58 — Pothole Protection
content_summary: Dingli JCPT0607A fault code 58 — Pothole protection device activated. Cause: uneven surface or edge detected by pothole guard sensors underneath the machine. This safety interlock prevents operation when the machine is not on level, stable ground. Solutions: (1) Move machine to a flat, stable surface away from edges, holes or ramps. (2) Inspect pothole guard sensors (proximity switches) beneath the platform for physical damage or obstruction. (3) Check sensor wiring and connectors for damage or loose connections. (4) Verify the pothole guard arms move freely and are not jammed. (5) If on flat ground and code persists, test sensor continuity and replace faulty sensor. Reset: push in and pull out the red Emergency Stop button after addressing the cause. Applies to Dingli JCPT0607A scissor lift.

title: Dingli JCPT1418RT ANSI Operator's Manual with Maintenance Information
content_summary: Official Dingli JCPT1418RT ANSI specification operator's manual with maintenance information. The JCPT1418RT is a rough-terrain scissor lift with engine power. Manual covers: (1) Safety rules and pre-operation inspection. (2) Operating procedures for driving and lifting. (3) Fault indicator readout — LED diagnostic display shows error codes; reset by cycling Emergency Stop button. (4) Maintenance schedule including hydraulic fluid, battery, and mechanical checks. (5) Fault code list with code numbers and descriptions covering ECU alarms, motor controller faults, pothole protection, overload, CAN bus timeout errors. This is the English-language ANSI version of the manual from UK dealer Advanced Access Platforms. Applies to Dingli JCPT1418RT rough-terrain scissor lift.

title: Fehlercodes Genie GS 3246 — Fault Code Document (German)

Genie scissor lifts use the GSDS (Genie SmartLink Diagnostic System) with 272 DTC codes and 6 OIC (Operation Indicator Codes). Key fault codes for GS-3246 include: Code 01 (ECM error), Code 02 (Platform ECM error), Code 12 (battery/power issue), Code 18 (pothole guard failure — check limit switches and wiring), Code 42-43 (coil errors), Codes 45-47 (sensor faults), Codes 52-58 (various sensor/switch errors), Code 68 (electrical fault). OIC codes: E1 (Emergency Stop activated), E4 (Low Battery — charge required), E6 (Tilt Sensor — machine on slope), E12/E13 (Control Communication fault — CAN bus). Reset: lower platform, turn off, engage override, restart. Document on Scribd.

DTC codes range from 01 to 68+ covering ECM errors, platform controller errors, coil faults, sensor faults, and communication errors. title: JLG Scissor Lift Troubleshooting Guide — Intella Parts
content_summary: Comprehensive JLG scissor lift troubleshooting guide covering common fault scenarios. Topics include: (1) Battery and electrical faults — check charge level, connections, fuses, and contactor operation. (2) Drive system faults — inspect motor brushes, check motor controller, verify brake release. (3) Lift system faults — hydraulic fluid level, pump operation, relief valve pressure, cylinder seals. (4) Fault code diagnosis — JLG uses a flash code system where the LED flashes in sequences (e.g., 3-3 = sensor fault, 4-4 = drive system). (5) Limit switch issues — check upper/lower limit switches for proper triggering. (6) Platform tilt/angle sensor errors — clean sensor, check wiring, recalibrate. (7) E-stop and interlock troubleshooting. Common models addressed: JLG 1930ES, 2030ES, 2630ES, 2646ES, 3246ES. Intella Parts is a JLG aftermarket parts supplier.

title: JLG 450A/AJ Series II Service Manual — Fault Codes Page 343 | ManualsDir
content_summary: JLG 450A and 450AJ Series II service manual, page 343 of 396, showing the complete fault code list (titled 'Fault Codes -33'). Fault code structure: first sequence = category (1=ECM, 2=platform, 3=sensor, 4=drive, 5=software), second sequence = specific fault within category. Key known codes: 3-3 (angle/tilt sensor fault), 4-4 (drive fault), 5-437 (software/calibration fault). Service manual covers complete electrical schematics, hydraulic diagrams, component testing procedures, and fault isolation trees. title: Scissor Lift Error Codes — ANSI Field Service Reference
content_summary: ANSI field service reference document for scissor lift error codes covering multiple brands. title: Genie GS Series Scissor Lifts Service and Repair Manual | Manuals.Plus
content_summary: Genie GS series scissor lift comprehensive service and repair manual hosted on Manuals.Plus. Covers multiple GS models. DTC codes cover ECM, platform ECM, hydraulic, battery, sensors, and control faults. Covers GS-1930, GS-1932, GS-2032, GS-2046, GS-2646, GS-3246. Includes CAN bus diagnostic procedures.

title: JLG Fault Code 43 — Forkliftaction Forum Discussion
content_summary: Forkliftaction forum discussion about JLG fault code 43 on aerial work platforms. Fault code 43 on JLG equipment relates to a driver output short circuit or solenoid valve fault. The forum thread covers: (1) Code 43 typically indicates a short circuit in a driver output — at least 50 troubleshooting paths may apply. (2) Diagnostic steps: check all solenoid valve coils for short circuits (should read 20-30 ohms resistance), inspect wiring harness for chafing or pinched wires, test each valve individually. (3) Common cause: failed solenoid valve coil creating a short to ground. (4) If harness tests OK, the controller (ECM/main board) may be at fault and require replacement. (5) Reset: cycle power after repair. Applicable to various JLG boom and scissor lift models using the JLG control system.

title: JLG Power Module Failure - Personality Range Error (9-9 / 99-5) | JLG Official
content_summary: JLG fault codes 9-9 or 99-5 on ES Scissors indicate Power Module Failure - Personality Range Error. The power module (PE) received an out-of-range value in its personality settings. Symptoms: machine fails to start, all functions unavailable. Cause: corrupted personality data in power module EPROM, or failed power module. Solution: (1) Cycle E-stop and key switch to attempt reset. (2) Connect JLG Analyzer (p/n 1001249695) to read full DTC. (3) If personality range error confirmed, reprogram or replace power module via JLG service. Models affected: ES Scissors series (1930ES, 2630ES, 3246ES, etc.).

title: JLG 3246 ES Scissor Lift Flash Code 2-5 Pothole Sensor Fault | JustAnswer
content_summary: JLG 3246 ES scissor lift fault code 2-5: Drive prevented due to pothole sensor issue. Code breakdown: first number (2) = platform control system, second number (5) = pothole/tilt fault. Cause: pothole bar not fully engaged or misaligned. Solution: (1) Inspect left and right pothole bars — both must be fully engaged and properly aligned. (2) Check pothole bar microswitches for continuity. (3) Inspect wiring harness to pothole switches for damage. (4) If bars are aligned but fault persists, check pothole switch adjustment. Reset: cycle E-stop after correcting pothole bar issue.

title: JLG T350 Flashing Code 9 - Service Required | Heavy Equipment Forums
content_summary: JLG T350 boom lift: Flash code 9 (single flash repeated) indicates machine requires service. Flash code 9 on JLG machines generally signals a system-level fault requiring diagnostic tool access. Procedure: (1) Release all E-stops. (2) Check footswitch engagement. (3) Verify machine is on level ground. (4) Connect JLG Analyzer for complete DTC reading — flash code alone insufficient for full diagnosis. (5) If analyzer unavailable, check battery voltage, inspect control module connections, and review error history if machine has display. JLG T350 uses 4WD articulating boom configuration; check swing/rotation sensor connections.

title: Control System Fault Codes - Genie Z-45/25 Service And Repair Manual Page 102 | ManualsLib
content_summary: Genie Z-45/25 articulated boom lift control system fault codes from official service and repair manual, page 102. The Z-45/25 uses Genie's standard fault code system with numeric codes covering electrical, hydraulic, sensor and control issues. Fault codes displayed on control module LED or display. General Genie fault code categories: E-codes for OIC (operational indicator), numbered DTC codes for device/circuit faults. Full service manual available on ManualsLib covers Z-45/25 hydraulics, electrical schematics, and complete troubleshooting procedures.

title: OIC and DTC Codes - Genie GS-2646AV Service And Repair Manual Page 92 | ManualsLib
content_summary: Genie GS-2646AV scissor lift Operation Indicator Codes (OIC) and Diagnostic Trouble Codes (DTC) from service manual page 92. OIC codes: LL (off level — do not raise), PHS (pothole protection not deployed). DTC codes from Genie SmartLink Diagnostic System (GSDS) covering 5 categories: hydraulic, battery/power, user interface, sensors/switches, and controls. title: Battery Charger Fault Codes and Flash Codes - JLG 1930ES Operator's Manual Page 58 | ManualsLib
content_summary: JLG 1930ES scissor lift battery charger fault codes and flash codes from operator's manual page 58. Battery charger LED flash patterns indicate: steady green = charging complete; flashing green = charging in progress; flashing red = charger fault. Common charger fault conditions: battery temperature out of range, battery voltage too low/high, charger overheating, bad cell detected. If charger fault: (1) Check battery water level. (2) Check battery connections for corrosion. (3) Verify AC input voltage. (4) Inspect charger connector. JLG 1930ES uses onboard 24V charger system.

title: OIC and DTC Fault Codes - Genie GS-1530/1532 Service And Repair Manual Page 180 | ManualsLib
content_summary: Genie GS-1530 and GS-1532 scissor lift complete OIC and DTC fault code table from service and repair manual page 180. Two code types: OIC (Operation Indicator Codes) for abnormal operating states, DTC (Diagnostic Trouble Codes) for device/circuit malfunctions detected by SmartLink GSDS. Key OIC codes: LL (off-level), PHS (pothole system). Key DTC categories: hydraulic coil faults, battery/power faults, user interface, sensor/switch faults, and control system. Full code chart with causes and corrective actions on page 180.

title: Genie GS-1930 Fault Codes Complete List and Fix Guide | Reliable Equipment Parts
content_summary: Complete Genie GS-1930/GS-1932 fault code list with descriptions and fixes. OIC codes: LL (off-level — lower platform, move to level surface), PHS (pothole switch fault). DTC codes: E1 (Emergency stop activated — check E-stop buttons), E4 (Low battery voltage — charge batteries, check connections), E6 (Tilt sensor activated — move to level ground, check tilt sensor), E12 (Platform-to-ground control communication loss — inspect CAN bus wiring), E18 (Height limit switch fault — check limit switch and wiring). Coil error codes: 52 (Forward coil), 53 (Reverse coil), 54 (Up coil), 55 (Down coil), 56 (Right coil), 57 (Left coil), 58 (Brake coil) — for coil faults: check coil resistance 20-30Ω, inspect wiring, check valve. Code 18: Pothole guard — check limit switches and harness. Code 68: Low voltage. Reset procedure: lower lift fully, turn off, use override mode, restart.

title: JLG Fault Code 211 - Power Cycle Required on Boom Lifts | GC Iron Support
content_summary: JLG boom lift fault code 211: Power cycle required. Appears on JLG articulating and telescopic boom lifts including 450AJ, 520AJ, 860SJ series. Code 211 indicates the control system requires a complete power cycle to clear a latched fault. Procedure: (1) Lower all boom functions to stowed/transport position. (2) Press and hold E-stop button. (3) Wait minimum 30 seconds for complete power discharge. (4) Release E-stop and restart with key. (5) If code 211 persists after power cycle, connect JLG Analyzer for underlying DTC. Common underlying causes: control module communication error, sensor data out of range, software fault latch.

title: DTC Introduction and Mast Sensor Default Code - JLG DSP M Operation Manual Page 93 | ManualsLib
content_summary: JLG DSP M mast-type vertical mast lift diagnostic trouble codes (DTC) from operation and safety manual page 93. Includes DTC system introduction and mast sensor default code documentation. JLG DSP M machines use coded diagnostic system: mast sensor faults display specific codes on the control panel. Mast sensor default code triggers when mast position sensor fails or reads out of range — machine disables lift function. Solutions: check mast sensor wiring, inspect sensor alignment on mast rail, verify sensor connector integrity. Full DTC list with corrective actions on page 93.

title: Comprehensive JLG Fault Codes Guide - JLG 660SJ and 860SJ | ManualsDump
content_summary: Comprehensive fault code guide for JLG telescopic boom lifts, specifically covering 660SJ, 860SJ, and E5002 models. JLG boom lift DTC format: two-part code (X-Y) where X identifies system group and Y identifies specific fault. Group 1 = ECM/engine, Group 2 = platform/basket, Group 3 = sensors, Group 4 = drive system, Group 5 = software/control. Code E5002: engine fault requiring service. Full analyzer-readable fault codes require JLG Analyzer (part 1001249695) connected to diagnostic port. For 860SJ: diagnostic port location is inside ground control box. Key service notes: after fault correction, always cycle power and verify code clears.

title: JLG Fault Codes List Ultimate Troubleshooting Guide | TractorDetailProblems
content_summary: Comprehensive JLG fault codes list covering all JLG MEWP models (scissor lifts, boom lifts, telehandlers). JLG DTC system overview: codes consist of two groups (system group-fault code), accessed via JLG Analyzer tool. Common fault groups: Group 1 (ECM/engine control), Group 2 (platform systems), Group 3 (sensors), Group 4 (drive/traction), Group 5 (control software). General troubleshooting sequence: (1) Note all active DTCs. (2) Start with highest group number first. (3) Check basics — battery, E-stops, footswitch. (4) Inspect wiring for corrosion and damage. (5) Use JLG Analyzer for live data monitoring. (6) Cycle power after each repair to verify code clears.

title: Analyzer Fault Code Listing - JLG 800S and 860SJ Service Manual Page 308 | ManualsDir
content_summary: JLG 800S and 860SJ telescopic boom lift complete analyzer fault code listing from service and maintenance manual, page 308. Contains all analyzer-readable DTC codes organized by group: Group 1 (ECM and engine control module codes), Group 2 (platform/basket controls), Group 3 (sensor faults — angle, pressure, height), Group 4 (drive system and traction), Group 5 (software and CAN bus). Example codes: DTC 001 (all OK), DTC 002 (ground mode OK), DTC 0010 (running at cutback). Requires JLG Analyzer (p/n 1001249695) connected to service port. Full DTC table with descriptions, probable causes, and corrective actions.

title: JLG 520AJ Service and Maintenance Manual 3121665 | Issuu

title: Fault Code 01-3 AWP Discussion | Forkliftaction Forum
content_summary: Forkliftaction forum discussion about fault code 01-3 on aerial work platforms. Code format X-Y: first digit (01/1) = ECM/engine control system group, second digit (3) = specific fault within that group. On JLG machines: code 1-3 typically relates to ECM sensor out of range or throttle position fault. Community discussion includes diagnostic steps, common causes, and repair procedures contributed by field technicians.

title: No Travel Condition - AWP Troubleshooting | Forkliftaction Forum
content_summary: Forkliftaction forum thread diagnosing no-travel fault condition on aerial work platforms. Common causes identified: pothole switch not engaged (require level ground), drive enable relay failure, battery voltage too low (<22V on 24V system), tilt switch activated, drive motor controller fault, faulty footswitch. Diagnostic sequence: (1) Check all E-stops released. (2) Verify footswitch operation. (3) Measure battery voltage. (4) Check pothole bars alignment. (5) Inspect drive enable relay. (6) Check for active fault codes. Applies to JLG ES scissors and boom lifts.

(4) Safety system faults — tilt sensor, pothole guards, E-stops, limit switches. (5) Fault code reading and interpretation. title: JLG Boom Lift Engine Trouble Code 520208:10 | JustAnswer
content_summary: JLG boom lift engine diagnostic code 520208:10 troubleshooting. Code 520208:10 is in J1939 SPN/FMI format: SPN 520208 is a manufacturer-specific parameter, FMI 10 indicates abnormal rate of change detected. Appears on JLG boom lifts with Deutz or Perkins engine (450AJ, 520AJ, 860SJ series). Diagnosis steps: (1) Check engine wiring harness for damage/corrosion. (2) Inspect ECM connector. (3) Verify engine sensor readings with JLG Analyzer. (4) Check for intermittent CAN bus communication faults. May indicate throttle position sensor, fuel system, or ECM communication issue. Clear code and retest after harness inspection.

Resolution often involves checking load-sensing hydraulic circuit pressure and boom lock valve.

title: Platform Leveling Fault — JLG 800S / 860SJ Service Manual Page 245 | ManualsDir
content_summary: JLG 800S/860SJ platform leveling fault diagnosis (service manual 3121259, page 245). Platform leveling fault triggered by: leveling cylinder open/short circuit, level sensor fault, hydraulic leveling valve fault. Diagnosis: (1) Check level cylinder solenoid resistance (20-30 ohm); (2) Inspect level sensor angle reading via JLG Analyzer; (3) Verify hydraulic pressure to leveling circuit. Reset: cycle E-stop after correction.

title: JLG 860SJ Drives Forward But Not Reverse — Analyzer Fault Code Diagnosis | JustAnswer
content_summary: JLG 860SJ telescopic boom lift: drives forward, not reverse. Diagnosis via JLG Analyzer: reverse drive solenoid fault or drive controller fault. Step-by-step: (1) Connect JLG Analyzer to read active DTC; (2) Check reverse drive solenoid coil resistance — spec 20-30 ohm; (3) Inspect wiring harness between controller and reverse solenoid for open circuit or short; (4) If solenoid OK, test drive controller output; (5) Swap controller if output signal absent. Also check: pilot pressure to reverse valve (min 250 PSI).

title: JLG 860AJ UGM Analyzer Module Not Communicating — Fault Diagnosis | JustAnswer
content_summary: JLG 860AJ boom lift: UGM (Universal Gateway Module) analyzer will not communicate. Diagnosis: CAN bus communication failure between UGM and machine ECU. Steps: (1) Check analyzer cable connection at diagnostic port; (2) Verify CAN bus termination resistors (120 ohm each end); (3) Inspect main CAN bus wiring for shorts or opens; (4) Check UGM module power supply; (5) Replace UGM module if bus wiring tests OK. Same procedure applies to 860SJ. UGM provides J1939 DTC and Deutz/Perkins engine fault access.

title: Error 6 - JLG fault code discussion | Forkliftaction Forum
content_summary: Forkliftaction forum thread discussing JLG Error 6 fault code on scissor and boom lifts. Error 6 is commonly an emergency stop circuit fault or interlock fault. Diagnosis steps: verify all E-stop buttons are released and functional, check interlock switches on platform gate/entry bar, inspect wiring harness for continuity from E-stop circuit to ECM. On some JLG models, Error 6 also relates to tilt sensor activation — confirm machine is on level surface. Reset by cycling key switch after resolving root cause. If error persists, check CAN bus node at platform control box.

Diagnostic procedure: observe LED display on ground control, identify 2-digit code, refer to fault code table for cause and corrective action.

title: Dingli JCPT1412DC Specs (2017-2025) | LECTURA Specs
content_summary: Technical specifications for Dingli JCPT1412DC wheeled scissor lift from LECTURA specs database. Production years 2017-2025. Key specs: 500kg load capacity, 14m working height (max 16m), DC electric motor drive, 24V battery system, platform size 2.8x1.2m, stowed height 2.3m, weight approximately 3000kg. The DC suffix indicates DC motor drive system, which uses a separate traction motor controller (TM1/TM2 in fault codes). The JCPT1412DC fault code system is documented in the operator's manual (Advanced Access Platforms PDF). Engine type: electric DC, controller: Curtis or Zapi typically used in this class.

title: JLG 3246ES Scissor Lift — Technical Overview | H-Equips
content_summary: Technical overview of JLG 3246ES electric scissor lift. The 3246ES features JLG's patented pothole protection system — fault code 2-5 (flash code) indicates pothole bars are misaligned or stuck. Common issues: pothole bar sensor wiring, pothole bar mechanical obstruction. Control system: JLG Multifunction Control Module (MFC) with CAN bus. Flash codes read via platform LED: first sequence = category, second = specific code. Code 3-3 = sensor fault, 4-4 = drive system fault. Battery maintenance critical: 24V 225Ah minimum, check specific gravity quarterly. The 3246ES uses same control platform as 3246RS (rough terrain variant).
